gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The content address of a `RecoveryPolicy` (RFC-0001 §4.6 / ADR-006 / `PolicyRef`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A deterministic BLAKE3 hash over the policy's canonical, sorted rules.  Stable across"] # [doc = " serialization boundaries and suitable as a cache / deduplication key."] pub type PolicyRef = ContentHash ;
gaps.reason: `type PolicyRef = ...` alias — Mycelium's `type_item` always introduces a new nominal sum type via `'=' constructor ('|' constructor)*`; a bare alias to an existing type would fabricate a sum type where none exists semantically

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An error computing the content address of a [`RecoveryPolicy`] (banked guard #5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returned by [`RecoveryPolicy::policy_ref`] when a fallback value cannot be serialized to a"] # [doc = " stable canonical form.  This is a configuration error — a fallback value type must implement"] # [doc = " [`serde::Serialize`] correctly (i.e. produce a stable, deterministic encoding) for the policy"] # [doc = " to be content-addressed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Never-silent (I1 / banked guard #5)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `policy_ref` refuses to produce a `PolicyRef` rather than silently skipping the offending"] # [doc = " value from the hash.  Skipping would cause two policies with different fallback values to"] # [doc = " produce identical `PolicyRef`s — a content-address collision (banked guard #5 violation)."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct PolicyHashError { # [doc = " The `serde_json` serialization error."] pub source : serde_json :: Error , }
gaps.reason: struct `PolicyHashError` has a field type with no confirmed mapping (qualified type path `serde_json :: Error` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5))

gaps.snippet: impl std :: fmt :: Display for PolicyHashError { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut std :: fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> std :: fmt :: Result { write ! (f , "cannot compute stable PolicyRef: fallback value serialization failed (serde_json): {}" , self . source) } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Display` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)

gaps.snippet: mycelium_std_core :: impl_std_error ! (PolicyHashError , source = | this | { Some (& this . source) }) ;
gaps.reason: item-position macro invocation — no macro system in this grammar

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A reified, content-addressed recovery policy."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Maps a **registry-resolved** [`ClassName`] to a [`RecoveryAction`].  Rules are stored in a"] # [doc = " `BTreeMap` (sorted by class name) so the content hash is deterministic and the policy is"] # [doc = " diffable / inspectable (C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN-ability (C3)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `RecoveryPolicy` is its own EXPLAIN artifact: the `rules()` iterator exposes every class →"] # [doc = " action binding, and [`RecoveryPolicy::policy_ref`] is the stable identity tag embedded in"] # [doc = " every [`crate::Resolution`] outcome."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Type bound"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `T` type parameter is the fallback value type — it must be"] # [doc = " [`serde::Serialize`] + [`std::fmt::Debug`] + [`Clone`] + `Send + Sync + 'static`."] # [doc = " `serde::Serialize` is required for stable, canonical content hashing of fallback values"] # [doc = " (banked guard #5 — `Debug` is not a stable serialization and is not used for hashing)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone)] pub struct RecoveryPolicy < T > { rules : BTreeMap < ClassName , RecoveryAction < T > > , }
gaps.reason: struct `RecoveryPolicy` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl < T > Default for RecoveryPolicy < T > { fn default () -> Self { RecoveryPolicy { rules : BTreeMap :: new () , } } }
gaps.reason: impl-level generic parameter(s) on a *trait-instance* impl (`impl<..> Trait for ..`) have no confirmed mapping yet — DN-130 (parametric trait-instance heads + coherence) is the unbuilt scope for that case; DN-131 authorizes only the inherent-impl slot

gaps.snippet: impl < T : Serialize + std :: fmt :: Debug + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static > RecoveryPolicy < T > { # [doc = " An empty policy (no rules)."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { RecoveryPolicy :: default () } # [doc = " Add an `on <class> => <action>` rule, resolving `class` **and** any `Escalate.to_class`"] # [doc = " through `registry` (X1 — both class names are registry-validated, never raw strings)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Replaces and returns any prior action for the class.  An unknown class (either the LHS"] # [doc = " `class` or an `Escalate` target) is an explicit configuration error ([`UnknownClass`]),"] # [doc = " never a silent fabrication (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`UnknownClass`] if:"] # [doc = " - `class` is not in the registry (LHS class), **or**"] # [doc = " - the action is [`RecoveryAction::Escalate`] and `to_class` is not in the registry"] # [doc = "   (X1 — the escalation target must be a registered class, never an unvalidated string)."] pub fn on (& mut self , registry : & ClassRegistry , class : & str , action : RecoveryAction < T > ,) -> Result < Option < RecoveryAction < T > > , UnknownClass > { let name = registry . resolve (class) ? ; if let RecoveryAction :: Escalate { ref to_class } = action { if ! registry . contains (to_class) { return Err (UnknownClass { name : to_class . clone () , }) ; } } Ok (self . rules . insert (name , action)) } # [doc = " The recovery action for a resolved class, if any."] # [must_use] pub fn action_for (& self , class : & ClassName) -> Option < & RecoveryAction < T > > { self . rules . get (class) } # [doc = " The rules in deterministic (class-sorted) order (C3 — inspectable, diffable)."] pub fn rules (& self) -> impl Iterator < Item = (& ClassName , & RecoveryAction < T >) > { self . rules . iter () } # [doc = " Whether the policy has no rules."] # [must_use] pub fn is_empty (& self) -> bool { self . rules . is_empty () } # [doc = " The **content address** of this policy (RFC-0005 `PolicyRef`; ADR-006)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A deterministic BLAKE3 over the canonical sorted rules; diffable and identity-stable."] # [doc = " Every outcome returned by [`crate::handle`] carries this `PolicyRef` when a rule was"] # [doc = " applied, so every outcome can be traced back to the exact policy that acted (C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Honesty (banked guard #5)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All hashed fields use stable, canonical encodings:"] # [doc = " - Class names are registry-resolved strings (X1)."] # [doc = " - Integer action parameters (`max_attempts`) are hashed as little-endian bytes."] # [doc = " - `EffectKind` values are hashed via their `Display` string."] # [doc = " - Fallback values are serialized with **`serde_json::to_vec`** — a canonical, deterministic"] # [doc = "   encoding that is stable across Rust versions and implementations.  `Debug` is **not** used:"] # [doc = "   `Debug` is not a stable serialization format and its output can vary between Rust versions"] # [doc = "   or custom implementations, making content-addressed identity unreliable."] # [doc = " - The rule count is hashed before iterating so a length-1 policy for class `\"ab\"` and a"] # [doc = "   length-2 policy for classes `\"a\"` / `\"b\"` do not collide."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`PolicyHashError`] if a fallback value cannot be serialized by `serde_json`."] # [doc = " This is a configuration error — a value type whose `serde::Serialize` impl fails"] # [doc = " (e.g. contains a `NaN` float that `serde_json` refuses to encode) cannot participate in"] # [doc = " content-addressed policy identity.  Returning `Err` rather than silently skipping the"] # [doc = " value is the only correct behavior: skipping would allow two policies with different"] # [doc = " fallback values to produce the same `PolicyRef` — a content-address collision (banked"] # [doc = " guard #5 / G2 — never silent)."] pub fn policy_ref (& self) -> Result < PolicyRef , PolicyHashError > { let mut h = blake3 :: Hasher :: new () ; let blob = | hasher : & mut blake3 :: Hasher , bytes : & [u8] | { hasher . update (& (bytes . len () as u64) . to_le_bytes ()) ; hasher . update (bytes) ; } ; blob (& mut h , b"mycelium.recovery-policy.v1") ; h . update (& (self . rules . len () as u64) . to_le_bytes ()) ; for (class , action) in & self . rules { blob (& mut h , class . as_str () . as_bytes ()) ; match action { RecoveryAction :: Fallback { value } => { h . update (& [0u8]) ; let bytes = serde_json :: to_vec (value . as_ref ()) . map_err (| e | PolicyHashError { source : e }) ? ; blob (& mut h , & bytes) ; } RecoveryAction :: Retry { max_attempts } => { h . update (& [1u8]) ; h . update (& max_attempts . to_le_bytes ()) ; } RecoveryAction :: Escalate { to_class } => { h . update (& [2u8]) ; blob (& mut h , to_class . as_bytes ()) ; } RecoveryAction :: CleanupThenPropagate { effect } => { h . update (& [3u8]) ; blob (& mut h , effect . to_string () . as_bytes ()) ; } } } let hex = h . finalize () . to_hex () ; Ok (ContentHash :: from_parts ("blake3" , hex . as_str ()) . expect ("blake3 hex is always a valid content hash")) } }
gaps.reason: impl type parameter `T` carries a bound with no confirmed mapping (a lifetime bound or another non-trait bound form) — DN-131 v1 covers plain trait-name bounds only

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The declared, closed effect set for a policy (I3 / RFC-0014 §4.5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns the set of `EffectKind`s that the policy's actions may perform.  Used by"] # [doc = " [`crate::effect::check_effects`] to enforce I3 (no undeclared effect)."] pub fn policy_effects < T > (policy : & RecoveryPolicy < T >) -> std :: collections :: BTreeSet < EffectKind > where T : Serialize + std :: fmt :: Debug + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static , { let mut set = std :: collections :: BTreeSet :: new () ; for (_ , action) in policy . rules () { match action { RecoveryAction :: Retry { .. } => { set . insert (EffectKind :: Retry) ; } RecoveryAction :: CleanupThenPropagate { effect } => { set . insert (effect . clone ()) ; } RecoveryAction :: Fallback { .. } | RecoveryAction :: Escalate { .. } => { } } } set }
gaps.reason: a `where` clause has no Mycelium equivalent
